I did NOT score this as a tele-1 violation: it is a real spec defect but lands in TELE-2's lane (Isomorphic Specification / Doc-Code Drift — the authoritative registry describing a reality the system does not implement), and it OVER-promises ABOVE tele-1's actual requirement. tele-1 mandates current-state durability + queryability (fully met by single-row UPDATE-in-place), NOT revision-history retention; the substrate is tele-1-conformant without it. Flagging it under tele-1 would be over-claiming out of lane. The other six sanctioned constructs: (2) ADR-024 §2.3 memory-mode 'state vanishes on process exit' — sanctioned dev/test-only, explicitly 'not a supported deployment target'; (3) substrate §2.4 LISTEN/NOTIFY 'drops messages if no consumer connected' — the durable truth is the table row, NOTIFY is a derived signal, handlers catch up via resource_version (truth never transient); unaudited: tele-13 (Director Intent Amplification) — Director-RATIFIED at HEAD on 2026-06-20 (docs/specs/vision-synthesis.md §9, full Mandate/Mechanics/Rationale/Faults/Success-Criteria) but it is absent from BOTH the audited corpus and the grid: vision-synthesis.md does not exist in the frozen snapshot 672bd0f, teles.md@SNAP holds only tele-0..tele-12, and the fan-out grid is hardcoded to tele-0..tele-12 (scripts/cdacc/spec-fanout.workflow.js line 22). No verdict exists or could be produced -> strictly unaudited. Compounding it: tele-13's sovereign-store instantiation (create_tele + add to teles.md + refresh tele-0's enumeration) is itself PENDING per §9 Disposition, so even at HEAD its spec↔state is unsettled and would itself be a finding.
underCovered: tele-0 (PASS / cited-obligation) — SHALLOW: the cited obligation is mechanical spec↔state parity, which passes clean, but a normative read of the snapshot's OWN teles.md fails. tele-0's Mechanics bullet 3 ('Every other tele (tele-1 through tele-10) composes...'), its Rationale ('The 10 orthogonal teles collectively compose...'), and Success-Criteria 1 & 2 ('tele-1 through tele-10' / 'tele-1..tele-10') all omit tele-11 and tele-12, which sit as ## Tele #11 / ## Tele #12 in the same snapshot file. The umbrella's account of its own constituents is incomplete; the PASS missed this surface. (This is a genuine pre-existing drift, teles.md dated Apr 25 and independently logged in vision-synthesis §7 — NOT one of the snapshot's planted FP-traps.)

tele-5 (PASS / asserted-interpretation) — NO CITED CLAUSE by the audit's own tier label. The mandate carries concrete, citable-or-measurable surfaces ('Director↔agent perception delta <1%', 'auto-hydrated context before cognitive loops') yet the verdict rests on interpretation rather than a ratified operationalizing clause or an evidence-of-practice cite. A PASS at the lowest spec tier is under-supported.

tele-10 (PARTIAL / asserted-interpretation) — DOUBLY WEAK: a gap-bearing verdict that is NOT anchored to any cited clause, so the PARTIAL is unlocated (we cannot tell WHICH obligation is only partially met). tele-10's Mechanics are concrete ('failure auto-spawns a Bug + post-mortem thread'; 'a single Director Approve executes the self-healing chain') and citable; the audit neither cited them nor pinned the gap to them.

tele-9 (PASS / cited-obligation) and tele-7 (PASS / cited-obligation) — SCOPE-AMBIGUOUS: these are strongly code/runtime-dominant (tele-9 'cannot prove under chaos in sandbox -> does not exist in production', 'sim↔prod delta <1%'; tele-7 runtime self-healing / no silent failures). The schema supports UNAUDITED for 'code-dominant (needs runtime reproduction = out of spec-altitude scope)', yet ZERO teles in the grid were marked UNAUDITED. A spec-altitude PASS here can only certify 'the obligation is specified', not behavioral conformance; without that scope stated it conflates map-specified with territory-conforms. tele-10 was appropriately humble (PARTIAL); tele-7/tele-9 got clean PASS with no UNAUDITED carve-out for the behavioral half.
offGrid: THE GESTALT LAYER IS ENTIRELY OUTSIDE THE GRID. The frozen corpus 672bd0f excludes docs/specs/vision-synthesis.md — the ratified 'binding narrative above the atomic teles'. So every property that lives ONLY at the synthesis altitude maps to no single tele AND is outside the audited scope: the four-strata composition (§2), the cross-tele seams, the meta-property (§3), the export/generalization vector (§5), tele-13 (§9), and the §7 known-drift finding. The sweep audits the atoms but never asks 'does the synthesis above the atoms hold' — the grid has no cell for it.

NORMATIVE-vs-MECHANICAL DRIFT CLASS (the CDACC thesis itself, demonstrated on tele-0). A per-tele 'cited-obligation' grid rewards spec==state parity and STRUCTURALLY cannot represent the case 'spec and state AGREE but are jointly stale / normatively wrong' (exactly tele-0's stale enumeration: Hub text == teles.md text, both omit tele-11/12/13, so mechanical parity passes clean). This is a limit of the instrument, owned by no tele — there is no grid cell for 'parity-clean but constitution-incomplete'.

CROSS-TELE SEAM / STRATA-COMPOSITION CONFORMANCE. Whether tele-4+tele-12 actually compose to deliver tele-5, and whether tele-1+tele-2 actually precondition the tele-3/correctness-engine, are first-class spec-altitude properties that belong to NO single tele. The fan-out audits each tele in isolation; the council's P8 cross-tele-correlation channel only fires on clustered PARTIAL/FAIL, and with a near-all-PASS spec vector there is nothing to cluster, so seam-conformance goes unasked at spec altitude.

THE META-PROPERTY / STRUCTURAL DOGFOOD (vision-synthesis §3): 'the system is built by the same agentic process it is building' and 'institutional memory is operational, not aspirational'. These are gestalt claims that span all teles and are owned by none; the per-tele grid cannot verdict 'is the dogfood structural' or 'does the ledger actually prevent re-learning'.
completeness.notes: Grounding: sweep audits frozen canary snapshot 672bd0f (2026-06-20 16:01, '[CDACC] wire P2 FULL fan-out'); grid hardcoded tele-0..tele-12 (scripts/cdacc/spec-fanout.workflow.js:22); tiers are spec-altitude evidence tiers (cited-obligation = clause cited; asserted-interpretation = lowest, no obligation cited) per the verdict schema + handoff P0. WITHIN the grid, all 13 cells have usable verdicts, so the only literally-unaudited tele is tele-13 (ratified at HEAD, excluded from corpus+grid). NOTE I dropped a candidate off-grid finding after verification: the CDACC instrument doc (docs/methodology/cdacc-dual-altitude-conformance-council.md) DOES exist in the snapshot (it is only absent from my working-tree vision-synthesis branch) — the two branches diverge (snapshot has the instrument but not the gestalt; HEAD has the gestalt but not the instrument), which is a corpus-fragmentation observation but not a clean off-grid finding. CAVEAT for the council: the snapshot contains intentional spec-interpretation FP-traps; the tele-0 staleness is a GENUINE drift (teles.md dated Apr 25, independently logged in vision-synthesis §7), not a planted canary — distinguish the two before scoring recall. Severity ranking of my findings: (1) tele-13 unaudited + gestalt-layer off-grid are the load-bearing gaps; (2) tele-0 underCovered is the highest-value missed surface because it is the worked proof of the normative-pass thesis; (3) tele-5/tele-10 asserted-interpretation and tele-7/9 missing-UNAUDITED are the audit's own self-flagged shallow spots.
